pattern: Mapping each day to +1 or -1 and tracking running prefix sums turns the count comparison into finding the widest span where the sum is positive, solvable by remembering the first index of each prefix value.
hints: Replace each day with +1 if it is tiring and -1 otherwise; the interval is well-performing exactly when its values sum to a positive number.
Work with prefix sums. If the current prefix is positive the whole prefix qualifies; otherwise you want the earliest earlier index whose prefix was exactly one less than the current.
Scan left to right keeping the first occurrence index of each prefix-sum value; when the running sum is positive the answer is i+1, else look up where prefix-1 first appeared.

edge_inputs.description: best interval is a prefix not the whole array
no tiring days means no valid interval
exactly eight hours is not tiring
all tiring days, whole array qualifies
balanced then a dip recovers a long suffix via prefix lookup

stress.description: long random hour log near the tiring threshold
stress.note: values cluster around 8 so tiring/non-tiring days mix densely
